desc: Swakopmund is Namibia's Atlantic desert hub where access is typically an international arrival plus domestic flight or 4-5 hour drive, best for adventure travelers and self-drive visitors who can handle sparse-service logistics.
hls: Signature: dune adventure circuit (sandboarding, quad biking, and Living Desert drives)
Signature: Walvis Bay lagoon cruises for dolphins, seals, and pelagic birdlife
Under-the-radar: Moon Landscape/Goanikontes outings beyond the main beachfront strip
Cooler coast base for pairing Skeleton Coast and inland Namib routes

todo.name: Living Desert tour
todo.desc: Signature guided drive focused on desert-adapted wildlife and tracking.

todo.name: Walvis Bay catamaran cruise
todo.desc: Signature marine outing for dolphins, seals, and seabird colonies.

todo.name: Dune 7 sandboarding or quad biking
todo.desc: Classic adrenaline session on major dune fields near Walvis Bay.

todo.name: Moon Landscape and Welwitschia drive
todo.desc: Under-the-radar geological route with dramatic gravel-valley vistas.

todo.name: Swakop River birding after fog clear
todo.desc: Under-the-radar morning stop where temporary wetlands attract birdlife.

todo.name: Mole/Jetty sunset walk
todo.desc: Low-effort evening coastal walk with cooler Atlantic air.

wifi.note: Stable in central hotels; weaker in desert-edge lodges.

prac.fltNote: UK/Ireland access usually requires one stop to Windhoek or Cape Town/Johannesburg plus a domestic hop or long drive; Namibian peak season (Jun-Oct) increases car-rental and flight demand.
prac.currency: Namibian dollar (NAD), with South African rand (ZAR) also widely accepted.
prac.lang: English is official; Afrikaans and German are also common in Swakopmund tourism businesses.
prac.tz: CAT (UTC+2) year-round; no daylight saving time.
prac.visa: Namibia entry policy varies by nationality, with a mix of visa-exempt and visa-required regimes; verify current Namibian immigration rules before booking.
alerts: Dense Atlantic fog can sharply reduce visibility on the B2/C34, causing convoy-slow driving and occasional delays.
Strong coastal winds can cancel or shorten marine cruises, scenic flights, and dune activity departures.
Long self-drive legs between fuel points and patchy mobile coverage require conservative fuel/water planning.

sweet: Jun-Oct for the driest, coolest adventure window and strongest wildlife-road-trip logistics; Mar-May and Nov are good shoulders for lower prices with mostly stable weather.
